note: Illustrative diagram of radiative frost on a highland kaukau mound. Not from the SPC dataset; no data claim, no source cited. The night-temperature curve is a schematic, not a station record.

indicators.traditional: Morning fog sits low in the valley and burns off late
indicators.satellite: Cold, clear nights show up as low night-time land-surface temperature

indicators.traditional: Cordyline (tanket) leaves at the garden edge pale and curl
indicators.satellite: Vegetation greenness dips over the high gardens

indicators.traditional: Creek levels drop and the stones stay dry by mid-morning
indicators.satellite: Rainfall runs below its normal range for the season

healthy.title: Cloud is a blanket
healthy.body: It is the clear nights that kill. With no cloud, the day’s heat radiates straight out to space and ground that normally never freezes can freeze by dawn.

frosted.title: No blanket came
frosted.body: A cloudless, windless night lets the surface cool far below the air a few metres up — the classic setup for radiative frost.

healthy.title: A living canopy
healthy.body: The vines close over the mound, holding the day’s warmth against the soil and shading the weeds.

frosted.title: Frost burn
frosted.body: Frost silvers the leaves overnight; they blacken and dry within days. The tuber below can survive, but with the canopy dead there is little left to feed it.

healthy.title: Why gardeners mound
healthy.body: Heaping the soil and folding in old vines to rot warms and drains it, and cold air slides off the crest downhill — a little height buys a little warmth.

frosted.title: Bricked ground
frosted.body: Weeks without rain bake a mound hard and it cracks; replanting needs soft, wet soil, so recovery has to wait for the rain.

healthy.title: A slow clock
healthy.body: From planting to harvest, sweet potato takes roughly five to nine months — what is eaten today was planted the better part of a year ago.

frosted.title: Two harvests at once
frosted.body: A killing frost takes the standing crop and the vine cuttings needed to replant, so the loss reaches months into the future.

healthy.title: Two ways of knowing
healthy.body: The garden gives its own signs — fog, leaf, creek — and satellites read the same conditions from orbit. Neither list replaces the other.

frosted.title: Both were right
frosted.body: The local signs and the instruments describe one event from two directions: a cold, dry, cloudless spell settling over the high gardens.
